back: Provides for the establishment, composition, appointments, qualifications, and removal procedure of the Supreme Court of India.
Declares the Supreme Court to be a 'Court of Record' with the power to punish for contempt of itself.
Empowers the President of India to consult the Supreme Court for advisory opinions on questions of law or public importance.
Mandates that there shall be a High Court for each State (or a common High Court for two or more states under Art. 231).
Grants High Courts the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights as well as any other ordinary legal rights.
A court whose acts and judicial proceedings are recorded for perpetual memory and testimony, holding the power to punish for civil or criminal contempt.
The legal right or capacity of a party to bring an action before a court. Relaxed significantly in Public Interest Litigation (PIL) cases.
The absolute last judicial remedy available in the Supreme Court to cure gross miscarriages of justice, created via the Rupa Ashok Hurra case (2002).
Discretionary power of the Supreme Court under Article 136 to grant special leave to appeal against any judgement/order from any court/tribunal (except court-martial).

front: Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)
back: Established under Article 148; known as the 'Guardian of the Public Purse', auditing all accounts related to expenditure from the Consolidated Fund of India and States.
A quasi-judicial body constituted under Article 280 every 5 years by the President to recommend vertical and horizontal tax revenue sharing between Centre and States.
Established under Article 324 as a permanent autonomous body responsible for conducting elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and President/Vice-President offices.
Appointed under Article 76 as the highest law officer in India; holds office during the pleasure of the President and advises the Union Government on legal matters.
Established under Article 315 as the central recruiting agency in India, serving as the watchdog of the merit system for All India and Central Services.
Appointed by the President of India after consultation with the CJI and Collegium of 4 senior-most SC judges (Article 124(2)).
Appointed by the President of India after consulting the Chief Justice of India, the Governor of the respective state, and the Chief Justice of that High Court.
Appointed, posted, and promoted by the Governor of the State in consultation with the State High Court under Article 233.

front: Appointment of State Public Service Commission Members
back: Appointed by the Governor of the State, holding office for 6 years or up to 62 years of age (Article 316).

front: Removal Ground for SC and HC Judges
back: Strictly limited to 'Proved Misbehaviour' or 'Incapacity' via an address supported by a Special Majority in both Houses of Parliament.

front: Removal of Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG)
back: Removed by the President in the EXACT same manner and on the exact same grounds as a judge of the Supreme Court of India.
Appointed by the Governor, but can ONLY be removed by the President following a Supreme Court inquiry report under Article 317.
Removed from office in the same manner and on the same grounds as a judge of the Supreme Court (Special Majority in Parliament).

front: Exclusive Original Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
back: Article 131: Authority to hear federal disputes between Centre vs State, Centre & State vs States, or between two or more States.
The legal opinion delivered by the Supreme Court upon Presidential reference is purely consultative and NOT legally binding on the President.
Article 227: Grants High Courts both administrative and judicial superintendence over all subordinate courts and tribunals in their state (except military tribunals).
The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age for Lok Sabha and Assembly elections from 21 years to 18 years (Article 326).
The Attorney General has the right to speak and take part in proceedings of either House or joint sittings, but has NO RIGHT TO VOTE.

front: First Chief Justice of Independent India
back: Justice H.J. Kania served as the inaugural Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of India when it was established on January 28, 1950.
Granted constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC) by inserting Article 338B into the Constitution.

front: High Court Judges' Salary vs Pension Charging Funds
back: High Court judges' salaries are charged on the Consolidated Fund of the STATE, whereas their pensions are charged on the Consolidated Fund of INDIA.
